Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lauren Jones, Esq. 415-926-7600 of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP for Plaintiff.
Elizabeth Hurwitz, Esq.415-357-0600 of Utrecht & Lenvin LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues/seeks clarification re: stay. Plaintiff responds (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff and CRoss-Defendant Cuong Van Tran's Motion To Compel Arbitration.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Cuong Van Tran's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted. Tran and Ramon and Natividad Paras-Nisce are required to arbitrate the disputes in the complaint as well as the declaratory relief claim in the cross-complaint based on the broad arbitration clause in their real estate purchase agreement. (Id. at 36.) Strong public policies favor arbitration and there is no proof that the separable agreement to arbitrate was procured by fraud. (Brown v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 938, 953, 958.) Also, I exercise discretion under CCP 1281.2(d) and 1281.4 to stay court prosecution of the claims against the Re/Max cross-defendants pending the arbitration's resolution. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
